ground_truth.title: Video Production Process: Complete Guide for 2025
ground_truth.author: Jacob Trussell
ground_truth.main_content:
Video dominates modern marketing. Programmatic video ad spend is expected to surpass $100 billion globally in 2025, driven by CTV advertising and short-form platforms. Consumers watch an average of 17 hours of online video weekly, and nearly 90% say it influences purchase decisions. Yet producing high-performing video marketing content at scale remains a hurdle for many teams.

Video production is the process of creating video content, encompassing five key stages: planning and development, pre-production, production, post-production, and marketing and distribution. Each stage builds on the last to deliver a polished, platform-ready creative that drives results.

The five stages of video production are:

Phase 1: Planning & Development - This is the phase in which the big questions and topics driving a video content shoot are posed and answered. What is the overall aim? Who is the target audience? How can you best express the content's aim and reach the audience? What resources do you have? Planning starts with macro questions that get progressively more granular.

Phase 2: Pre-Production - The questions surrounding 'how will this get made?' take on a more practical edge. This includes hiring personnel and crew, location scouting, budget planning, and determining shoot duration. During pre-production, teams rely on pre-visualization techniques like storyboards and shot listing.

Phase 3: Production - This involves the actual shoot, which is highly controlled as it involves talent and crew on the clock. Despite the best-laid plans, there are often hurdles to deal with, whether logistical issues like weather or performance challenges.

Phase 4: Post-Production - This involves everything required to make your video look and sound polished after the shoot, including editing, color correction, visual effects, and sound design. Post-production can allow you to repurpose production content to work in several ways.

Phase 5: Marketing & Distribution - Now achieved with the help of data and targeting. You should have a good idea of where your audience lies and ensure content is crafted to connect with them.

Video production costs vary significantly based on project scope. Simple UGC-style ads start under $2,000. Mid-tier live-action spots range $5,000-$50,000. High-end animation can cost over $15,000 to $50,000 per minute. However, AI generation tools now deliver comparable quality for fractions of traditional costs.

Best practices for video production include testing multiple creative variations, incorporating accessibility from the start, building feedback loops with stakeholders, optimizing lighting and audio for all devices, and documenting processes for future efficiency.
